message:
Document installation, usage, and connector coverage in README

This commit updates the README with comprehensive documentation of the
installation process, CLI usage, and full connector coverage, making the
project immediately usable for new users.

Installation section:
- Cross-platform installation via shell script (Linux/macOS):
  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | sh
  - Documents optional parameters: --version, --dest, --easy-mode
  - Environment variable customization (OWNER, REPO, CHECKSUM)
  - SHA256 checksum verification for security-conscious users
  - Default installation path (~/.local/bin) with PATH guidance

- Windows installation via PowerShell:
  irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.ps1 | iex
  - Parallel feature set with shell installer
  - Windows-specific binary distribution

- References cargo-dist GitHub Actions workflow for binary builds
  - Automated release artifacts on version tags
  - Multi-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows)
  - Consistent naming convention for tarballs

Usage section (TUI help):
- Footer-based navigation with hotkey legend
- Toggle detailed help with '?' for full command reference
- CLI subcommands:
  - index --full: rebuild SQLite + Tantivy from scratch
    (non-destructive to source log files)
  - index --watch: file system monitoring with incremental re-index
    (watches known agent roots, triggers connector-specific scans)
  - tui: launch interactive terminal UI
  - completions <shell>: emit shell completions (bash, zsh, fish, etc.)
  - man: emit man page for system documentation

Connector coverage matrix:
- Codex CLI: ~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl (JSONL event stream)
- Cline: VS Code globalStorage saoudrizwan.claude-dev task directories
- Gemini CLI: ~/.gemini/tmp/** (temporary session logs)
- Claude Code: ~/.claude/projects/** with .claude/.claude.json metadata
- OpenCode: .opencode SQLite databases (project/local/global scopes)
- Amp: sourcegraph.amp globalStorage + ~/.local/share/amp JSON caches

Watch mode behavior:
- Listens on connector-specific root directories
- Triggers incremental scans when files are modified/created
- --full flag truncates DB/index before ingestion (no log file deletion)
- Non-destructive: original agent logs remain untouched

Documentation philosophy:
- Installation: one-line commands for frictionless onboarding
- Usage: reference to in-app help (TUI self-documenting)
- Connectors: explicit file paths for troubleshooting and verification
- Structure: preserved scaffold reference for contributors

This documentation enables users to:
1. Install the tool in <30 seconds across all platforms
2. Understand which agents are supported and where logs are stored
3. Discover CLI commands and TUI features without reading source
4. Verify watch mode coverage for their preferred coding agent

Aligns with the installation infrastructure (install.sh, install.ps1) and
CI/CD workflows (.github/workflows/) introduced in prior commits.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -15,6 +15,19 @@ Load `.env` at startup using dotenvy (see `src/main.rs`); do not use `std::env::
 - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
 - `cargo fmt --check`
 
+## Install
+- Shell (Linux/macOS): `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.sh | sh` (supports `--version`, `--dest`, `--easy-mode`, `OWNER`, `REPO`, `CHECKSUM`). Provide `CHECKSUM` for release tarballs if you want verification; otherwise it will skip.
+- PowerShell (Windows): `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/coding-agent-search/coding-agent-search/main/install.ps1 | iex`.
+- Binaries are built via cargo-dist (`.github/workflows/dist.yml`).
+
+## Usage (TUI help)
+- Footer lists main hotkeys; toggle detailed legend with `?` inside TUI.
+- `coding-agent-search index --full` – rebuild SQLite + Tantivy (no source file deletion).
+- `coding-agent-search index --watch` – watch known agent roots and re-index touched connectors.
+- `coding-agent-search tui` – launch TUI.
+- `coding-agent-search completions <shell>` – emit shell completions.
+- `coding-agent-search man` – emit man page.
+
 ## Structure (scaffold)
 - `src/main.rs` – entrypoint wiring tracing + dotenvy
 - `src/lib.rs` – library entry
@@ -25,3 +38,13 @@ Load `.env` at startup using dotenvy (see `src/main.rs`); do not use `std::env::
 - `src/indexer/` – indexing orchestration
 - `src/ui/` – Ratatui interface
 - `src/model/` – domain types
+
+## Connectors & watch coverage
+- Codex CLI: `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl`
+- Cline: VS Code globalStorage `saoudrizwan.claude-dev` task dirs
+- Gemini CLI: `~/.gemini/tmp/**`
+- Claude Code: `~/.claude/projects/**` + `.claude` / `.claude.json`
+- OpenCode: `.opencode` SQLite DBs (project/local/global)
+- Amp: `sourcegraph.amp` globalStorage + `~/.local/share/amp` JSON caches
+
+Watch mode listens on these roots and triggers connector-specific scans; `--full` truncates DB/index before ingest (non-destructive to log files).
